mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures that force equity valuation multiples to contract, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 6.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms robust aggregate demand transmission across the supply chain, driving earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model when RSAFS and INDPRO move in opposite directions, or forward returns remain flat despite concurrent positive growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses short-term equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 5-day returns when the federal funds rate is already at the effective lower bound, or the signal reverses direction during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or the signal consistently underperforms during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 900,000 units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter as downstream spending collapses.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where HOUST breaches the 900.0 threshold, or housing weakness fails to transmit to broader equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while payroll employment stagnates indicates productivity gains that boost corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The productivity divergence signal yields negative or flat forward returns when accompanied by rising input costs, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion that validates earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 60-day returns when accompanied by deteriorating leading indicators, or the signal reverses during stagflationary environments.
